title: Agent Core Loop & Turn Management

actors.name: User
actors.description: Human interacting via CLI, frontend, or task file; sends queries and receives streaming responses

actors.name: ReflectScript
actors.description: Autonomous monitoring script (loaded via --reflect); calls check() on interval and triggers agent tasks

actors.name: TaskFile
actors.description: File-based I/O actor for one-shot task mode (--task); writes input.txt, reads output.txt

actors.name: LLMBackend
actors.description: External LLM service (Claude, OAI, etc.) accessed via ToolClient/NativeToolClient; produces responses and tool_calls

actors.name: OS/Shell
actors.description: Subprocess/file-system executor for code_run, file_read, file_write, web_execute_js tools

use_cases.name: Submit Query
use_cases.description: Enqueue a user query with a per-task display_queue; slash-commands are pre-processed by _handle_slash_cmd

use_cases.name: Run Agent Turn Loop
use_cases.entry_point: agent_runner_loop(client, sys_prompt, user_input, handler, tools_schema, max_turns=40)
use_cases.description: Core generator loop: iterates up to max_turns; calls LLM, parses tool_calls, dispatches each, assembles next message

use_cases.name: Call LLM for Response
use_cases.description: Sends accumulated messages + tools schema to LLM; yields streamed content; returns response with optional tool_calls

use_cases.name: Dispatch Tool Call
use_cases.description: Resolves do_{tool_name} method; fires tool_before_callback, executes tool generator, fires tool_after_callback; returns StepOutcome

use_cases.name: Run Code
use_cases.description: Executes python/bash/powershell snippet; supports inline_eval; returns stdout/stderr as StepOutcome.data

use_cases.name: Ask User Question
use_cases.description: Pauses loop and prompts user for input; returns StepOutcome with should_exit=True after answer

use_cases.name: Web Scan / Execute JS
use_cases.description: Retrieves simplified HTML of current browser tab or executes arbitrary JS via CDP bridge

use_cases.name: Handle Turn End
use_cases.entry_point: BaseHandler.turn_end_callback(response, tool_calls, tool_results, turn, next_prompt, exit_reason)
use_cases.description: Called after all tool calls in a turn; GenericAgentHandler override updates history_info and working memory; returns next_prompt string fed back to LLM

use_cases.name: Read / Write File
use_cases.description: File I/O operations; paths resolved relative to cwd (temp/); StepOutcome wraps content or success/error

use_cases.name: Update Working Checkpoint
use_cases.description: Persists key_info into handler.working dict; used across sessions to carry forward context

use_cases.name: Handle No-Tool Response
use_cases.description: Terminal step when LLM responds without any tool call; triggers exit or done-hooks in _done_hooks list

use_cases.name: Start Long-Term Update
use_cases.description: Initiates background memory consolidation into global_mem.txt / global_mem_insight.txt

use_cases.name: Abort Agent
use_cases.description: Sets stop_sig=True and appends to handler.code_stop_signal; GeneraticAgent.run() loop breaks on next chunk check

use_cases.name: Switch LLM Backend
use_cases.description: Rotates llmclient; transfers backend.history; reloads tool schema (CN variant for glm/minimax/kimi)

use_cases.name: Load Tool Schema
use_cases.description: Reads assets/tools_schema[suffix].json; replaces 'powershell'→'bash' on non-Windows; sets global TOOLS_SCHEMA

use_cases.name: Handle Slash Command
use_cases.description: Pre-processes /session.key=val and /resume before enqueuing; mutates llmclient.backend attributes directly

key_relationships: GeneraticAgent aggregates BaseHandler (via GenericAgentHandler instance per task)
GenericAgentHandler extends BaseHandler — implements do_* tool methods + turn_end_callback
agent_runner_loop depends_on BaseHandler.dispatch → produces StepOutcome per tool call
StepOutcome controls loop: should_exit=True breaks loop, next_prompt=None signals CURRENT_TASK_DONE
GeneraticAgent.run() iterates agent_runner_loop generator — streaming chunks to display_queue
Tool schema (TOOLS_SCHEMA) is a module-level global; reload triggered on LLM switch or every 10 turns
flow_summary: 1. Actor calls put_task → query queued with display_queue
2. run() dequeues: builds sys_prompt (get_system_prompt + global_memory), creates GenericAgentHandler
3. agent_runner_loop entered (generator): turn counter starts, max_turns=40
4. Each turn: client.chat() → response + tool_calls parsed
5. For each tool_call: handler.dispatch() → tool_before_callback → do_{tool} → tool_after_callback → StepOutcome
6. StepOutcome.should_exit=True → exit_reason=EXITED; no next_prompt → CURRENT_TASK_DONE; else continue
7. turn_end_callback assembles next message with tool_results; loop continues
8. Loop exits on: max_turns, should_exit, no_tool with empty _done_hooks
9. run() collects full_resp from generator chunks; puts {'done': full_resp} to display_queue
important_notes: agent_runner_loop is a generator (yield-based streaming); callers iterate it for intermediate chunks
BaseHandler.dispatch uses yield-from (try_call_generator) to support generator-based tool methods
History is stored inside *Session (LLMBackend), not in messages list — messages is always single-turn
Tool reset: client.last_tools='' every 10 turns and on unknown tool to prevent context bloat
source field in put_task ('user','feishu','task','reflect') affects injection of anchor_prompt
done_hooks (_done_hooks list) allow post-task follow-up prompts before loop exit
Abort is cooperative: stop_sig checked per chunk; code_stop_signal list checked inside code_run
